title: armeria marginata
intro: Armeria marginata, also known as the Sea Thrift or Thimbleweed, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects. The health benefits of Armeria marginata include soothing respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Armeria marginata is often prepared for herbal use in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
